report_metadata.report_title: PRC State-Sponsored Cyber Actor Living off the Land to Evade Detection

tools.name: certutil
tools.evidence_snippet: The actor has used the following commands to identify additional opportunities for obtaining credentials in the environment [T1555], [T1003]: dir C:\Users\{REDACTED}\.ssh\known_hosts

tools.name: dnscmd
tools.evidence_snippet: The actor has used the following commands to identify additional opportunities for obtaining credentials in the environment [T1555], [T1003]: dnscmd . /enumrecords /zone {REDACTED}

tools.name: ldifde
tools.evidence_snippet: The actor also used the following commands to identify additional opportunities for obtaining credentials in the environment [T1555], [T1003]: ldifde.exe -f c:\windows\temp\cisco_up.txt -p subtree

tools.name: makecab
tools.evidence_snippet: The actor also used the following commands to identify additional opportunities for obtaining credentials in the environment [T1555], [T1003]: makecab ..\backup\210829-020000.zip

ttps.evidence_snippet: The actor has exploited vulnerabilities [T1190] in widely used software including, but not limited to: CVE-2021-40539—ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus.
The actor has used webshells for persistence and exfiltration [T1505.003], with at least some of the webshells derived from the Awen webshell.
The actor has used compromised Small-Office Home-Office (SOHO) devices (e.g. routers) to obfuscate the source of the activity [T1090.002].
The actor has used the following commands to enumerate the network topology [T1016], the active directory structure [T1069.002], and other information about the target environment [T1069.001]: arp -a
The actor has used the following commands to identify additional opportunities for obtaining credentials in the environment [T1555], [T1003]: wmic volume list brief

summary: This joint Cybersecurity Advisory (CSA) highlights a recently discovered cluster of activity associated with a People’s Republic of China (PRC) state-sponsored cyber actor, also known as Volt Typhoon. The advisory provides an overview of hunting guidance and associated best practices to detect this activity.

timeline.event: First known exploitation of CVE-2021-40539 (ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus)
timeline.evidence_snippet: Earliest evidence of exploitation of CVE-2021-40539 occurred on May 27, 2023, when threat actors began targeting ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus installations.
